Hero.description: CosmoKit brings GPS routes, deep links, push payloads, proxy testing, and capture tools into one native Mac workspace.
Hero.subtext: Free tier available · macOS 14+ · Requires Xcode
Hero.heroImageAlt: CosmoKit's main workspace visual showing the native macOS control panel, simulator tools, and the ultimate productivity dashboard for iOS developers

Features.label: Features
Features.title: Everything you need, {highlight}
Features.subtitle: Replace dozens of {command} commands with an intuitive interface.

screenshot.title: Screen Capture
screenshot.description: Grab clean screenshots from the selected simulator and keep capture settings close to the rest of your testing workflow.

video.title: Recording
video.description: Record simulator sessions without jumping through Terminal commands, then save the result right from the same workspace.

proxy.title: Network Proxy
proxy.description: Inspect requests, introduce delays, simulate failures, and validate edge cases with a built-in proxy workflow.

push.title: Push Payload Testing
push.description: Queue test notifications, preview how they render on-device, and verify custom payload data before shipping.

deeplinks.title: Deep Links
deeplinks.description: Store reusable URL schemes and universal links so common app flows stay one click away during debugging.

gps.title: Locations and Routes
gps.description: Build route presets, replay movement, and coordinate changes across your simulator tests without leaving the panel.

apps.title: Multiple Simulators
apps.description: Run and control more than one simulator from the same workspace while keeping runtime tools, appearance, and app controls close by.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Flip simulator appearance and related UI settings while you test states side by side.

statusbar.title: Status Bar
statusbar.description: Adjust simulator presentation details like status bar output when a test or capture needs a cleaner frame.

permissions.title: Permissions
permissions.description: Reset and manage app permissions — camera, location, notifications — without reinstalling.

faceid.title: Face ID
faceid.description: Simulate enrollment, match, and mismatch for biometric authentication testing.

keychain.title: Keychain
keychain.description: Browse and manage keychain items stored by your app in the simulator.

Gallery.label: Gallery
Gallery.title: See it in action
Gallery.subtitle: A native macOS interface designed for speed and clarity.

Pricing.label: Pricing
Pricing.title: Simple, {highlight} pricing
Pricing.subtitle: Start free, upgrade when you need more. All plans include every future update.

proFeatures.f11: Simulator tools (appearance, status bar, permissions, Face ID, keychain)

Pricing.footnote: All plans are managed through the Mac App Store. Cancel or switch anytime.

Downloads.subtitle: Download CosmoKit from the Mac App Store and take full control of your iOS Simulator.
Downloads.helperDescription: Install this signed helper to let CosmoKit configure macOS HTTP/HTTPS proxy settings in one click. Subscriptions and Pro access stay inside the Mac App Store app.

FAQ.label: FAQ
FAQ.title: Frequently asked questions
FAQ.subtitle: Everything you need to know about CosmoKit.

q1.question: What is CosmoKit?
q1.answer: CosmoKit is a native macOS app that provides a unified control panel for the iOS Simulator. It replaces dozens of terminal commands (xcrun simctl) with an intuitive graphical interface — screenshot capture, video recording, push notifications, deep links, GPS simulation, network proxy, app management, and more.

q2.question: What are the system requirements?
q2.answer: CosmoKit requires macOS 14 (Sonoma) or later and Xcode installed on your Mac. It works with all iOS Simulator runtimes supported by your Xcode version.

q3.question: What's included in the free tier?
q3.answer: The free tier includes screenshot and video capture (with watermark), push notifications, deep link testing, GPS location simulation, and 5 saved entries per tool. It supports one simulator at a time.

q4.question: What does CosmoKit Pro unlock?
q4.answer: Pro removes all watermarks, unlocks unlimited simulators, adds device frames and bezels for screenshots, audio recording, touch indicators, custom aspect ratios, network proxy, full app management tools, simulator appearance/status bar/permissions/Face ID/keychain controls, location routes, and unlimited saved entries.

q5.question: How does billing work?
q5.answer: All subscriptions are managed through the Mac App Store. You can choose monthly (R$ 2,99/mo), yearly (R$ 19,99/yr — save 44%), or a one-time lifetime purchase (R$ 69,99). You can cancel or switch plans anytime through your Apple ID settings.

q6.question: Is CosmoKit sandboxed?
q6.answer: Yes, CosmoKit is distributed through the Mac App Store and runs in a sandboxed environment. It communicates with the iOS Simulator through approved system APIs and xcrun simctl commands.

q7.question: Does it work with physical devices?
q7.answer: CosmoKit is specifically designed for the iOS Simulator. It does not connect to physical iOS devices — it focuses on making simulator-based development and testing as efficient as possible.

q8.question: How do I get support?
q8.answer: You can reach us at contato@usecosmoskittool.com or through the in-app feedback option. We actively respond to all support requests and feature suggestions.

Footer.description: The ultimate macOS companion for iOS developers. Simulator testing made easy.
